validated_product_model.validated_positioning: Source-Blendr is a supplier product-and-service aggregation platform for resale businesses that need to convert vendor data, service costs, bundles, recipes, and margin rules into customer-ready quotes, catalogs, price books, or proposals.
validated_product_model.provisional_beachhead: Apparel decorators and promotional product businesses
validated_product_model.mvp_wedge: Product plus service aggregation into margin-aware quote or price book output.
deferred_layers: Deep ecommerce, CRM, accounting, and storage integrations

positioning_analysis.primary_differentiation: Combines supplier products, services, production recipes, cost breakdowns, margins, and customer-facing outputs in one workflow.
weak_claims: Complete operating system for every resale business is too broad for MVP.
Automatic supplier synchronization should not be promised until supplier access is validated.
AI pricing and quote automation should be assistive, not authoritative, until validated.

strategic_differentiation: Product-plus-service aggregation rather than product-only sourcing.
Reusable bundles and production recipes rather than simple catalog line items.
Margin-first quote and price book generation.
Stageable connector, AI, analytics, and portal layers.

confidence_rationale.market: Adjacent markets and industry platforms validate demand for sourcing, catalog, quote, and workflow software.
confidence_rationale.user: User pains are realistic based on adjacent platforms and Stage 1 workflows, but still need primary interviews.
confidence_rationale.competitive: Competitors validate the category and create clear differentiation pressure.
confidence_rationale.technical: Core MVP is feasible if supplier sync and AI are deferred.
confidence_rationale.operational: Workflow is feasible with import templates and simple approvals.
confidence_rationale.business: Paid adjacent tools support viability, but Source-Blendr-specific pricing is unvalidated.
confidence_rationale.scalability: Scalable if connectors, AI, and document generation are modular and staged.
confidence_rationale.architecture_readiness: Sufficient for Stage 3 because unresolved items can be represented as architecture constraints rather than blockers.

architecture_constraints: Design supplier connectors as adapters and implement file/manual import first.
Separate supplier source records from normalized asset records.
Represent products, services, bundles, recipes, pricing rules, costs, margins, generated outputs, customers, and quote states as explicit domains.
Keep AI, integrations, portals, analytics, and enterprise governance behind modular expansion boundaries.
Apply secrets management and least-privilege principles before any credential-stored supplier integration.

validation_recommendations: Proceed to Stage 3 with a constrained MVP and architecture warnings.
Use apparel decorators/promotional product businesses as provisional architecture examples.
Architect for quote output first, with catalog and price book as related generated-output variants.
Do not implement customer portals, AI automation, or deep integrations as launch-critical.

completion_status.reason: Market, technical, business, and operational assumptions meet minimum readiness thresholds when MVP scope is constrained and high-risk expansion areas are carried forward as architecture warnings.
next_actions: Run Stage 3 system architecture using the constrained MVP and Stage 2 architecture constraints.

completion_status.reason: Validated assumptions and mitigated risks are sufficient for architecture progression.
